title: require-after-commands accepted (RFC 5228 §6.1)
description: RFC 5228 §6.1 mandates that all require statements MUST appear before any other command. compile() scans all stmts (not just leading ones) for require declarations, with no ordering check. A script like 'keep; require ["fileinto"]; fileinto "x";' is accepted when it must be rejected. Fix: during the require-collection loop, reject any non-require statement seen before the first require is encountered, or equivalently verify all require stmts appear only at the head of the parsed script.
